flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 6b inapplicable (conjecture is salvageable); this is Case 3-style derivation defect but spans the program definition, the lemma, and the main theorem so it exceeds stage_4d scope. Regression pattern (a): second-round reject sharpens the first-round 'missing closure/measurability hypotheses' flag into a concrete falsity — K_x in the conditional program enforces only p_a(x) and r_a(x), so Lemma lem:conditional-program's iff and Theorem thm:sharp's reverse inclusion are false against the locked full observable law P=law(X,A,Y). Fix is a Stage-0 re-derivation, not a localized patch. No Bucket A: P is already locked in the parameter block, so adding the missing factual-distribution constraints encodes the existing contract, it does not strengthen it.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of offline contextual-bandit policy value under joint misspecification of the logging propensity and the outcome model, with bounded f-divergence between the misspecified and true propensity and a bounded sup-norm gap on the outcome regression, recovering doubly-robust point identification at the trivial bounds and Manski-Pepper-style truncated outcome bounds without unconfoundedness
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains the PartialID substrate rather than a duplicate proposal: ThmSmith/doc/API.md records no existing PartialID proposal for offline contextual-bandit policy value under joint propensity and outcome misspecification. AutoID doc/API.md §9j and §9j3 provide Manski-family and Balke-Pearl-style bounded-outcome/finite-LP substrate, but they do not state a contextual-bandit policy-value identified interval with pointwise f-divergence logging distortion, an arm-mean tube, and full factual reward-cell matching.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are DudikLangfordLi2011 and WangAgarwalDudik2017 for the DR point-ID corner; KallusZhou2018, KallusZhou2020, KallusZhou2021, HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026, IshikawaHe2023, and ZhangXuTan2025 for confounding-robust or simultaneous-sensitivity policy/value bounds; and Manski1990 for the vacuous bounded-outcome corner. The v6 gap is the finite-grid sharp identified set where the same latent response table must match the full observed law P(A,Y|X), satisfy a pointwise f-divergence logging budget, and satisfy a sup-norm nominal arm-mean tube."}
seed_list: Prior work DornGuoKallus2025 would imply that sharp sensitivity can be made doubly robust around nuisance misspecification. Our seed conjectures that contextual-bandit policy values under simultaneous propensity-divergence and outcome-regression budgets have a non-rectangular sharp interval because the two worst cases must use the same latent response table.

Prior work KallusMaoWangZhou2022 would imply that DR ideas extend cleanly to f-divergence robust OPE. Our seed conjectures that latent logging-confounding f-divergence produces a different kinked population interval because the divergence acts on assignment conditional on response types rather than on deployment distributions.

Prior work IshikawaHe2023 would imply that sharp lower bounds for confounded offline contextual bandits can be organized through conditional moment constraints. Our seed conjectures that adding a bounded outcome-regression gap creates a coupled mean-band constraint that changes the endpoint geometry, not just the estimator.

Prior work DudikLangfordLi2011 would predict accuracy when one nuisance model is good. Our seed conjectures that when both nuisance models are only boundedly wrong, the sharp policy-value interval is asymmetric around the DR score because residual sorting interacts with target-policy weights.

Prior work WangAgarwalDudik2017 would predict that reward-model inconsistency is mainly a minimax MSE and SWITCH truncation issue. Our seed conjectures that at the population level there is a misspecification-budget threshold where the sharp identified set changes slope, giving SWITCH-like truncation an identification interpretation.

Prior work MastenPoirierZhang2024 would imply that sensitivity to unconfoundedness is naturally a scalar bound curve. Our seed conjectures that offline policy value needs a two-budget breakdown surface whose acceptance region can be non-rectangular because propensity and reward deviations are jointly feasible or infeasible.

Prior work ManskiPepper2000 would imply that shape restrictions sharpen bounded-outcome intervals. Our seed conjectures that a sup-norm tube around a reward regression acts like a data-adaptive shape restriction whose interaction with propensity divergence creates truncated policy-value bounds not captured by monotone restrictions.

Prior work SiZhangZhouBlanchet2020 would predict that distributional ambiguity around logged data can protect offline contextual-bandit learning. Our seed conjectures that logging-confounding ambiguity is not exchangeable with context-distribution shift because observed factual-arm equations constrain only the former.

Prior work LeePaik2024 would imply that estimated logging policies can be handled by variance-optimal DR corrections when logging is correctly specified. Our seed conjectures that bounded divergence misspecification can reverse a target-policy ranking even in regimes where the estimated-logging DR variance is optimal.

Prior work Tan2006 would imply that bounded odds-ratio sensitivity is the canonical way to relax propensity ignorability. Our seed conjectures that general f-divergence sensitivity in multi-action bandits creates an action-simplex obstruction absent from binary odds-ratio quantile balancing.

Prior work JinXuZhengZhou2025 would imply that MSM robust welfare criteria are adequate for confounding-robust policy learning. Our seed conjectures that robust welfare is not sharp under joint model misspecification unless the outcome-regression tube is included, and the tube can discontinuously change the robust optimal policy.

Prior work Manski1990 would imply that no-assumption bounded outcomes give simple fill-in bounds. Our seed conjectures that these fill-in bounds are the continuous vacuous-budget endpoint of a sharper joint sensitivity curve, enabling an interpretable breakdown threshold from DR point ID to no-assumption bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DornGuoKallus2025 would imply that sharp sensitivity can be made doubly robust around nuisance misspecification. Our seed conjectures that contextual-bandit policy values under simultaneous propensity-divergence and outcome-regression budgets have a non-rectangular sharp interval because the two worst cases must use the same latent response table.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn, Guo, and Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp ATE sensitivity bounds with double sharpness and double validity under bounded unmeasured confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not merely weakening an assumption; predicts a strict non-rectangularity and shared-latent-table phase transition absent from separate sensitivity results.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DornGuoKallus2025 because double sharpness already handles nuisance misspecification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their target is ATE under an odds-ratio MSM, not contextual-bandit policy value with a simultaneous f-divergence logging budget and sup-norm reward-regression tube.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KallusMaoWangZhou2022 would imply that DR ideas extend cleanly to f-divergence robust OPE. Our seed conjectures that latent logging-confounding f-divergence produces a different kinked population interval because the divergence acts on assignment conditional on response types rather than on deployment distributions.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus, Mao, Wang, and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Proposes doubly robust DROPE and DROPL for KL and general f-divergence uncertainty sets.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the seed separates two named ambiguity geometries and predicts a kinked sharp interval, not an extension of DROPE to another setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just DROPE with a different uncertainty set.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: DROPE perturbs environment distributions, while this seed perturbs the latent logging kernel subject to observed factual-arm equations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work IshikawaHe2023 would imply that sharp lower bounds for confounded offline contextual bandits can be organized through conditional moment constraints. Our seed conjectures that adding a bounded outcome-regression gap creates a coupled mean-band constraint that changes the endpoint geometry, not just the estimator.

anchor_paper.author: Ishikawa and He
anchor_paper.claim: Gives a sharp lower-bound estimator for offline contextual bandits under unobserved confounding and f-divergence conditional moment constraints.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the new phenomenon is coupled endpoint geometry under a reward-regression tube, not applying their method to another estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from IshikawaHe2023 by adding linear constraints to their program.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only partly: adding the constraints is feasible, but the strict non-rectangular phase transition and DR/Manski dual calibration are not in their statement.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DudikLangfordLi2011 would predict accuracy when one nuisance model is good. Our seed conjectures that when both nuisance models are only boundedly wrong, the sharp policy-value interval is asymmetric around the DR score because residual sorting interacts with target-policy weights.

anchor_paper.author: Dudik, Langford, and Li
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces DR policy evaluation and learning for contextual bandits, accurate when either the reward or logging model is good.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not deriving a bias bound for DR; it predicts sharp asymmetric partial-ID intervals around the DR corner.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a standard DR bias formula with bounded nuisance errors.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: a bias formula is not sharp partial identification without unconfoundedness and does not enforce compatibility with observed factual outcomes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WangAgarwalDudik2017 would predict that reward-model inconsistency is mainly a minimax MSE and SWITCH truncation issue. Our seed conjectures that at the population level there is a misspecification-budget threshold where the sharp identified set changes slope, giving SWITCH-like truncation an identification interpretation.

anchor_paper.author: Wang, Agarwal, and Dudik
anchor_paper.claim: Establishes minimax OPE rates in agnostic contextual bandits and proposes SWITCH for bias-variance tradeoff.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the proposed phase transition is an identification boundary, not an estimator variant or target-version of an ATE result.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a population restatement of SWITCH truncation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: SWITCH is an estimator with MSE analysis; the seed concerns the exact identified interval under latent compatibility constraints.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MastenPoirierZhang2024 would imply that sensitivity to unconfoundedness is naturally a scalar bound curve. Our seed conjectures that offline policy value needs a two-budget breakdown surface whose acceptance region can be non-rectangular because propensity and reward deviations are jointly feasible or infeasible.

anchor_paper.author: Masten, Poirier, and Zhang
anchor_paper.claim: Estimates treatment-effect bound functions under scalar relaxations of unconfoundedness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the non-rectangular two-budget surface is a regime-change phenomenon, not a scalar-sensitivity extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just multidimensional sensitivity analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the seed makes a sharp feasibility claim about joint latent response tables, not merely plotting two scalar relaxations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ManskiPepper2000 would imply that shape restrictions sharpen bounded-outcome intervals. Our seed conjectures that a sup-norm tube around a reward regression acts like a data-adaptive shape restriction whose interaction with propensity divergence creates truncated policy-value bounds not captured by monotone restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Shows monotone instrumental-variable and shape restrictions can sharpen partial-identification bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it predicts a different truncation geometry from a named shape-restriction framework, not just a weaker assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski-Pepper by treating m0 as a shape restriction.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Manski-Pepper monotonicity does not encode f-divergence logging constraints or target-policy weighted factual-arm compatibility.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SiZhangZhouBlanchet2020 would predict that distributional ambiguity around logged data can protect offline contextual-bandit learning. Our seed conjectures that logging-confounding ambiguity is not exchangeable with context-distribution shift because observed factual-arm equations constrain only the former.

anchor_paper.author: Si, Zhang, Zhou, and Blanchet
anchor_paper.claim: Develops distributionally robust policy evaluation and learning for offline contextual bandits under environment ambiguity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the obstruction is a named mismatch between two ambiguity geometries, not extending DRO to another shift family.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already known because DRO sets can encode any ambiguity.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: generic DRO can be specified broadly, but the named Si et al. object does not impose observed potential-outcome compatibility under hidden logging.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LeePaik2024 would imply that estimated logging policies can be handled by variance-optimal DR corrections when logging is correctly specified. Our seed conjectures that bounded divergence misspecification can reverse a target-policy ranking even in regimes where the estimated-logging DR variance is optimal.

anchor_paper.author: Lee and Paik
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces DRUnknown for OPE with estimated logging policy, optimal when logging and value models are correctly specified.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the seed predicts policy-ranking reversal under bounded misspecification, not a variance refinement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an estimation paper and ranking reversal is just bias under misspecification.
seed_details.rebuttal: The bias observation is known, but a sharp partial-ID ranking-reversal threshold under f-divergence and outcome tubes is not supplied there.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Tan2006 would imply that bounded odds-ratio sensitivity is the canonical way to relax propensity ignorability. Our seed conjectures that general f-divergence sensitivity in multi-action bandits creates an action-simplex obstruction absent from binary odds-ratio quantile balancing.

anchor_paper.author: Tan
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces a distributional/propensity-score approach to causal sensitivity analysis through bounded hidden-confounding influence.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the multi-action simplex obstruction is a boundary/regime phenomenon, not simply replacing odds ratio by f-divergence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine generalization from binary treatment to multi-action policies.
seed_details.rebuttal: False if the conjectured obstruction holds: multi-action f-divergence budgets couple action probabilities on the simplex and change endpoint geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work JinXuZhengZhou2025 would imply that MSM robust welfare criteria are adequate for confounding-robust policy learning. Our seed conjectures that robust welfare is not sharp under joint model misspecification unless the outcome-regression tube is included, and the tube can discontinuously change the robust optimal policy.

anchor_paper.author: Jin, Xu, Zheng, and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Develops robust and efficient policy learning under unobserved confounding using MSM robust welfare criteria.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the discontinuous robust-policy change is a named downstream phenomenon, not applying their method to another target.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is policy learning, while the proposal is only evaluation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Evaluation intervals are the population objective for policy learning; a sharpness failure can change which policy robust learning selects.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 would imply that no-assumption bounded outcomes give simple fill-in bounds. Our seed conjectures that these fill-in bounds are the continuous vacuous-budget endpoint of a sharper joint sensitivity curve, enabling an interpretable breakdown threshold from DR point ID to no-assumption bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Gives nonparametric bounded-outcome treatment-effect bounds without point-identifying assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is a calibration and breakdown-threshold phenomenon, not simply restating Manski bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: The endpoint is exactly Manski and therefore not new.
seed_details.rebuttal: The endpoint is intentionally not new; the proposed contribution is the sharp path and threshold between DR point ID and Manski fill-in.

proposed_from.literature_map: DudikLangfordLi2011 (ICML, https://arxiv.org/abs/1103.4601) establishes DR contextual-bandit policy evaluation when either the reward model or logging model is good; tension: it is point-identifying and does not give a population interval when both are bounded wrong. WangAgarwalDudik2017 (ICML, https://proceedings.mlr.press/v70/wang17a.html) gives minimax agnostic OPE and SWITCH; phenomenon: reward misspecification is handled statistically, not as a sharp identified set. SiZhangZhouBlanchet2020 (ICML, https://proceedings.mlr.press/v119/si20a.html) develops DRO policy evaluation under environment shifts; gap: ambiguity is over deployment distributions, not latent logging-confounding laws. KallusMaoWangZhou2022 (ICML, https://proceedings.mlr.press/v162/kallus22a.html) gives DR DROPE under KL/general f-divergence sets; tension: DR estimation for DRO is not sharp partial ID under hidden assignment. Tan2006 (JASA) introduces marginal sensitivity via bounded propensity odds; anchor for propensity-distortion budgets. DornGuo2023 (JASA, https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2022.2069572) derives sharp IPW sensitivity bounds and shows coarser relaxations are too wide; open gap: paired outcome-regression misspecification. DornGuoKallus2025 (JASA, https://ideas.repec.org/a/taf/jnlasa/v120y2025i549p331-342.html) gives doubly valid/doubly sharp ATE sensitivity estimators; closest robustness thread, but not contextual-bandit policy value with a reward-model tube. IshikawaHe2023 (AISTATS, https://proceedings.mlr.press/v206/ishikawa23a.html) studies confounding-robust offline contextual bandits and f-divergence conditional moments; gap: no paired sup-norm outcome model budget. MastenPoirierZhang2024 (JBES, https://doi.org/10.1080/07350015.2023.2183212) estimates sensitivity bound functions over a scalar relaxation; useful model for reporting a curve. JinXuZhengZhou2025 (arXiv, https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.20550) learns policies under MSM robust welfare; consumer for a sharper joint evaluation interval. Manski1990 (AER) and ManskiPepper2000 (Econometrica) supply bounded-outcome and shape-restricted partial-ID anchors; calibration corner is Manski fill-in.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 ACCEPT, Stage 0.5 REVISE x3 on novelty: kernel collapsed to subfield (standard Balke-Pearl finite latent + rectangular no-go); reviewer flagged that field-tier negative result needs a named published estimator/workflow that uses the refuted rectangular endpoint. Math sound. Also triggered intervention-parser bug (logged in PIPELINE_NOTES).
